prompt: Read كَتَبَ aloud and write its three short vowels.
expected_answer: ka-ta-ba: three fatḥas, one on each consonant.
accepted_variants: all three vowels are fatha (a a a)
fatha fatha fatha

prompt: Read رَبّ aloud and name the diacritic on the bāʾ.
expected_answer: rabb: a fatḥa plus a shadda on the bāʾ (the bāʾ is doubled).
accepted_variants: fatha then shadda on the ba; the ba is doubled
shadda marks the doubled ba
forbidden_answers: there is a sukun on the ba

prompt: Give the one-word English gloss of مِنْ.
accepted_variants: it means from

prompt: Which is 'did not' and which is 'why': لَمْ / لِمَ? Name the deciding feature.
expected_answer: لَمْ means did not; لِمَ means why. The harakah on the second letter (the lām) distinguishes them.
accepted_variants: lam is did not, lima is why; the vowel on the lam decides
decided by the kasra vs fatha on the lam
required_reasoning: harakah named as the deciding feature

prompt: Is مُعَلِّمَة masculine or feminine, and how do you know?
expected_answer: Feminine — the tāʾ marbūṭa ة marks the feminine.
accepted_variants: feminine, by the ta marbuta ending
feminine because of the ة
required_reasoning: ta marbuta named as the marker

prompt: Give the attested broken plural of بِنْت.
accepted_variants: banat
the broken plural is بنات

prompt: Is يَنْصُرُ past or present, and what is its root?
accepted_variants: present tense, root n-s-r
present, the root is نصر

prompt: True or false: رَسُول means 'to send'. Explain.
expected_answer: False — رَسُول is a noun meaning 'messenger'; a noun never takes a verb gloss like 'to send'.
accepted_variants: false; rasul is a noun, messenger, not a verb
false, it is the noun messenger
required_reasoning: rasul identified as a noun meaning messenger

accepted_variants: door of the house
the house's door
required_reasoning: rendered as door of the house (idafa)

prompt: What does إِلَيْنَا mean, and what is its root?
expected_answer: It means 'to us'; root أ ل ي — not ل ي ن ('soft').
accepted_variants: to us, root a-l-y
means to us; the root is الي not لين
forbidden_answers: it is from the root lyn meaning soft
required_reasoning: to us given as the meaning
root given as a-l-y and not lyn

prompt: Is الْبَيْتُ كَبِيرٌ nominal or verbal? Name the subject.
accepted_variants: nominal sentence, subject is al-bayt
nominal; mubtada is the house

prompt: Is كَتَبَ الطَّالِبُ الدَّرْسَ nominal or verbal? Name the doer and the object.
expected_answer: Verbal; the doer (fāʿil) is الطَّالِبُ and the object (mafʿūl) is الدَّرْسَ.
accepted_variants: verbal sentence; doer is the student, object is the lesson
verbal; fail is at-talib, maful is ad-dars

prompt: مَنْ vs مِنْ: which is 'who' and which is 'from', and what tells them apart?
expected_answer: مَنْ means 'who'; مِنْ means 'from'. The harakah on the content letter (fatḥa vs kasra) separates them.
accepted_variants: man is who, min is from; the vowel on the mim decides
man=who, min=from, told apart by the harakah
required_reasoning: harakah named as the deciding feature

prompt: إِنَّ vs أَنَّ: gloss each and name the deciding feature.
expected_answer: إِنَّ means 'indeed/verily'; أَنَّ means 'that'. The hamza seat distinguishes them.
accepted_variants: inna is indeed/verily, anna is that; the hamza seat decides
inna=verily, anna=that, told apart by the hamza
required_reasoning: hamza seat named as the deciding feature

prompt: You hover a word and the plaque is blank. What does that mean, and what do you do?
expected_answer: The word has not yet earned a certified, authored gloss — parse it by procedure (ṣarf/nahw); do not guess.
accepted_variants: it is not yet certified; parse it by procedure and do not guess
not certified, work it out by sarf and nahw without guessing
forbidden_answers: the word is broken, ignore it
required_reasoning: parse by procedure and do not guess

prompt: A hover for جَادَلُوكَ says only 'to argue'. What visible information is missing?
expected_answer: The plural subject marker and the attached second-person masculine singular object كَ ('you') — they dispute with you, not a bare infinitive.
accepted_variants: the plural doer and the attached you (ka) object are missing
missing the plural subject and the 2nd-person masc sg object suffix
required_reasoning: plural subject marker named as missing
attached you (ka) object named as missing
